title: silene latifolia subsp. alba
intro: Silene latifolia subsp. alba, also known as White Catchfly, is a flowering plant species within the Silene genus, characterized by its white flowers and broad leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, Silene latifolia subsp. alba is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to benefit from its medicinal properties.
